habitat: Forest edges; dry/wet forests; tea plantations; wooded savannas; mangroves; and gardens
story: Meet the Crested Serpent Eagle (Spilornis cheela), a distinctive bird from the Accipitridae family. Preferring habitats such as Forest edges; dry/wet forests; tea plantations; wooded savannas; mangroves; and gardens, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Least Concern (LC), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Crested Serpent Eagle serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Crested Serpent Eagle enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
